channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Gretzky witnesses Ovechkin moving 1 goal back of his record as he joins the chase in person
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Wayne Gretzky witnessed Alex Ovechkin scoring his 893rd goal to move one back of tying his NHL record, as the Canadian great made his first in-person appearance during this chase.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anaheim mayor wants Los Angeles Angels to change their name
item.description: In an open-letter about the Angels future, the Anaheim mayor called for the team to be named for the city it actually represents.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Imanaga and Happ lead the Cubs past the Padres 3-1 in Wrigley Field opener
item.description: Shota Imanaga pitched one-run ball into the eighth inning in Chicago's home opener at Wrigley Field, and the Cubs handed San Diego its first loss with a 3-1 victory over the Padres on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts will make White House visit after skipping previous celebration under Trump
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers shortstop Mookie Betts said he will attend next week's World Series championship celebration at the White House after he declined to attend a previous ceremony during President Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cardinals make Trey McBride highest-paid tight end in NFL history with 4-year contract extension
item.description: The Cardinals signed tight end Trey McBride to a four-year contract reportedly worth $76 million, which makes McBride the highest-paid tight end in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MLW star Brett Ryan Gosselin looks to give Rogue Horsemen championship gold at Battle Riot VII
item.description: Brett Ryan Gosselin has a different attitude these days, and his place in the Rogue Horsemen has something to do with it. He's looking to win some gold at Battle Riot VII.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doug Bruno 'feels great,' but says stepping aside is the right call as Jill Pizzotti takes over at DePaul
item.description: CHICAGO – Less than a week after Hall of Fame head coach Doug Bruno stepped down for health reasons, the DePaul women’s basketball program named his successor—and it’s a familiar name to fans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Chicagoland foursome heads to Augusta for Drive, Chip and Putt Finals
item.description: GOLF, Ill. - Mastering golf can take a lifetime. Luckily, these four started early. "When I was like 2, I was holding a plastic club, watching a golf tournament - I don't remember which one," remembered 12-year-old Winnetka native Jack Kemper.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Teaching Chicago's hard-working values, basketball legend Tom Kleinschmidt builds a dynasty at DePaul Prep
item.description: DePaul College Prep's boys' basketball team, coached by Tom Kleinschmidt, has won three consecutive IHSA championships, thanks to his hard work, discipline, and respect, which he learned growing up on the city's Northwest Side.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: 2025 Augusta National Women's Amateur
item.description: The 2025 Augusta National Women's Amateur is underway at Champions Retreat Golf Club in Evans, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, Fox 5

item.title: Vejmelka makes 33 saves as Utah HC stays in playoff hunt
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah is not out of the playoff hunt just yet. Karel Vejmelka made 33 saves in his 18th consecutive start in goal, as the Utah Hockey Club stayed alive in the chase for the NHL playoffs with a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Calgary Tuesday night at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Caleb Williams' development, Bears search for "weapons" and Kyler Gordon's contract extension with The Athletic's Kevin Fishbain
item.description: The Chicago Bears are looking to develop Caleb Williams and use their top draft picks to bolster their offense, while also focusing on adding a dynamic running back and working on a contract extension for Kyler Gordon.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Curtis Granderson talks Cam Smith and Kyle Tucker, Will Venable and the Sox identity, torpedo bats
item.description: Cubs' top prospect Cam Smith is set to make his MLB debut this season, while the White Sox are off to a respectable start with a 2-2 record, and the Yankees have introduced torpedo bats, which are technically operating within the guidelines of the current rule book but are causing outrage among fans
item.dc:creator: Chris Boden, WGN 9

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Auburn completes sweep of No. 1 seeds into Final Four, beating Michigan State 70-64
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Johni Broome held his injured right arm through most of Auburn’s Elite Eight postgame celebration. That didn’t keep the star forward from climbing a ladder to cut down the net he then wore around his neck.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Diamondbacks rally for 8 runs in the eighth to beat the Cubs 10-6, split 4-game series
item.description: Lourdes Gurriel Jr. hit a game-tying, two-run homer, Josh Naylor had the go-ahead RBI double and the Arizona Diamondbacks used an eight-run eighth inning to rally past the Chicago Cubs 10-6 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: RSL gets shut out again at Minnesota United, 2-0
item.description: ST. PAUL (ABC4 Sports) - For the second straight game, and third overall this MLS season, Real Salt Lake failed to score a goal in a 2-0 shutout at Minnesota United Saturday night. Tani Oluwaseyi recorded a brace to help Minnesota United hand Real Salt Lake its fourth loss of the season. Dayne St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC loses to defending champs in OT, 2-1
item.description: SUNRISE, Fla. (AP) - Sam Bennett scored his second goal of the game off an assist from Brad Marchand at 4:18 of overtime to give the Florida Panthers a 2-1 win over the Utah Hockey Club at Amerant Bank Arena on Friday. By earning one point, Utah is now nine points behind St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fans chant 'sell the team' at Rays home opener; team owner says talks are nothing new
item.description: The Rays pulled out a win in their new home for the season at Steinbrenner Field, but the ball club's future in Tampa Bay remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC
